real_questions.text: Walk us through NII and dividend coverage.
How comfortable are you in realizing residual value on assets at end of lease, and what supports that?
How does demand for your products hold up in volatile trading environments?
What is the growth trajectory in data and retail-oriented offerings?
Will oil market volatility lead to higher fee revenue in energy products — and in equity options and rate products on your platform?
What is happening with origination and refinance volumes — can you continue to originate and grow the business?
How do you plan to maintain high trading volumes in quieter markets?

strong_answer_markers.text: Knows volume and fee sensitivity to commodity volatility by product line.

agency_intel.text: Dividend coverage and NII are strong signals of BDC health — expect both. Anchors differ by agency; know which anchor your lead agency uses and lead with those metrics.
For leasing companies, the residual value realization story matters more than asset diversification for its own sake — assets failing to hold residual value directly hurts performance and earnings.

agency_intel: For exchanges and clearinghouses: the clearing fee stream is incredibly resilient from a credit perspective and most rated entities sit in the A category. The emerging threat is stablecoin and digital-asset payment networks — expect the disruption question and have a strategic answer.

agency_intel.text: For non-bank mortgage finance, the volume outlook is the opening conversation — agencies want assurance the company can continue to originate and grow.
